item.title: DataNexus Architecture: Four Layers and Two Flows
item.description: Why ontology-based routing instead of a single NL2SQL pipeline — and what the second flow, from Pull to Push, looks like

item.title: 4. SCD - When a Customer Moves, Where Were Past Orders Shipped?
item.description: When dimension data changes, do you overwrite history or preserve it? We implement SCD Type 1, 2, and 3 differences in SQL, then build a production pattern with dbt snapshot.

item.title: 3. Silver Layer - Promoting Bronze to an Analysis-Ready State
item.description: Cleanse and standardize the raw data stacked in Bronze. Fix types, unify column names, remove duplicates. We define this process as SQL models in dbt.

item.title: 2. Bronze Layer - Load the Source Data Exactly As-Is
item.description: There are two ways to load data into Bronze. Overwrite everything, or bring only what changed. Which one you choose completely changes the complexity of your pipeline.

item.title: 1. Medallion Architecture - Why We Stack Data in Three Layers
item.description: Bronze, Silver, Gold. What changes when you load data into separate layers. We build it hands-on with DuckDB and dbt.

item.title: Fixing AI UI Design with Lazyweb MCP
item.description: Lazyweb MCP provides AI agents with access to 250,000 real-world app screens to improve generated UI/UX quality for free.

item.title: Modularizing Document Expertise for the GitHub Trending Era
item.description: A legacy software firm hit #1 on GitHub by open-sourcing its PDF extraction engine, shifting from monolithic products to high-performance AI infrastructure.

item.title: Building Cross-Validation Systems with Claude Code and Codex
item.description: A dual-agent strategy separating author and reviewer roles to eliminate code blind spots and improve developer productivity.

item.title: Prompt Master: Engineering Waste-Free Claude Workflows
item.description: Reduce AI retries and token waste using a structured framework that optimizes prompts across nine technical dimensions and specific model architectures.

item.title: Optimizing RAG Accuracy Through Structural Data Engineering
item.description: Improve RAG retrieval quality by preserving document hierarchy and layout context through smart chunking and Markdown conversion.

item.title: Scrapling: Adaptive Web Scraping That Survives UI Changes
item.description: Scrapling tracks elements by similarity rather than fixed selectors, bypasses anti-bot detection natively, and connects to AI agents via MCP – built for web environments that change without warning.

item.title: Hermes WebUI: Browser-Based Control for Autonomous Agents
item.description: Hermes WebUI moves autonomous agent management from the terminal to the browser – multi-profile context isolation, file browsing, and long-term memory in one interface.

item.title: Managing a Stock Portfolio from the Terminal with firma
item.description: firma replaces spreadsheet-based portfolio tracking with a local SQLite store, Finnhub price feeds, and MCP integration for Claude Desktop – letting you query your investments in plain language.

item.title: ChatGPT Personalized Reports as Shareable Image Cards
item.description: One photo or one conversation log is enough for ChatGPT to produce a personalized report as a shareable image card. A collection of prompts you can use right now.

item.title: 10. Spider 72%: The Dataset, Not the Model, Shapes the Error Profile
item.description: Switching to Pro actually dropped accuracy by 8%p. I categorized 19 errors into 6 types and ran the same pipeline on Spider. The distributions were completely different. DB characteristics determine the error profile more than the model does.

item.title: 9. BIRD 56%: Nine Experiments and What Got Ruled Out
item.description: I hit 80% on my own 30-question benchmark, but only 56% on BIRD Mini-Dev’s 50 public questions. Nine experiments later, I had ruled out the multi-candidate hypothesis from three different angles. What’s left is schema understanding and methodology.

item.title: 5. AEO - Why Coding Agents Read Documentation Differently
item.description: If GEO optimizes for consumer AI, AEO optimizes for coding agents. This article covers document length constraints, llms.txt, skill.md, and AGENTS.md — the files that matter.

item.title: What Actually Improves Claude Code Performance: Configuration and Architecture
item.description: Keeping Claude Code minimal, forcing full reasoning with three settings.json lines, and investing in system architecture — a practical take on what actually moves the needle.

item.title: 8. 30-Question Retail Sample at 80%: Four PDCA Cycles
item.description: After wiring up the router, I ran a 30-question benchmark and pushed NL2SQL EX (Execution Accuracy) from 66.67% to 80%. Here’s what I fixed across four cycles and where things broke.

item.title: 7. When a Question Comes In, Who Decides the Routing?
item.description: The term definitions are done. But when a user asks a question, who decides whether to search the graph, write SQL, or run a vector search? Things I ran into while designing the router.

item.title: 6. When You Don't Have to Build Agent Infra Yourself, Harnesses Become Obsolete. What About the Ontology?
item.description: Shortly after the Conway leak, Anthropic officially launched Claude Managed Agents. As agent infrastructure gets absorbed into platforms, here’s why DataNexus’s ontology layer remains safe.

item.title: 32 Slash Command Shortcuts That LLMs Instantly Understand
item.description: 32 slash commands that work out of the box with Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini – no custom definitions needed. Categorized by use case with practical combination strategies.

item.title: Analyzing the Official GitHub Repository of a Major Korean Brokerage Open API
item.description: A structural analysis of the official sample code from a major Korean brokerage API, optimized for LLM agents and Python environments.

item.title: macshot: A Native macOS Tool Emerging as an Alternative to Paid Apps
item.description: A look at macshot, a native open-source macOS screenshot and screen recording tool that delivers powerful features without the subscription burden.

item.title: 5. Automating Metadata Maintenance: Karpathy's LLM Wiki Architecture
item.description: RAG starts from scratch every time. Karpathy proposes having the LLM maintain a wiki directly so knowledge accumulates. DataNexus’s ontology catalog needs the same principle to avoid abandonment.

item.title: 4. Off-Site GEO - How to Win Over AI That Ignores Your Official Site
item.description: Even with perfect On-Site GEO, half of AI citations come from external channels. We cover platform-specific Off-Site strategies and how to diagnose your robots.txt setup.

item.title: 3. On-Site GEO Technical Architecture - From Product DB to JSON-LD
item.description: How product master DB data flows through a 3-stage pipeline to become JSON-LD in your HTML <head>. Covers the pipeline architecture and SSR-based automated deployment.

item.title: OpenDocuments: A Local RAG Platform That Unifies Fragmented Team Knowledge
item.description: An open-source platform that connects documents scattered across Notion, GitHub, and S3, then queries them with a local LLM. Runs entirely on-premise without external APIs.

item.title: Thinking Like an Agent Conductor in the AI Era
item.description: Even after adopting agents, if the work structure itself does not change, productivity does not scale. Reid Hoffman’s conductor strategy starts by breaking that illusion.

item.title: Using Korean Legal Data for AI Agent Development via Beopmang API
item.description: Parsing raw legal XML means broken table structures and half a day lost on preprocessing. Beopmang is an API that delivers pre-cleaned JSON, solving that problem upfront.

item.title: Optimizing Large-Scale Excel Downloads in Spring Boot
item.description: A Spring Boot library for streaming 1M-row Excel downloads without OOM. A single annotation eliminates the boilerplate.

item.title: OpenDocuments: A Self-Hosted RAG Platform Connecting Scattered Team Knowledge
item.description: A self-hosted RAG platform that runs on Ollama to unify team documents scattered across Notion, GitHub, and S3. Natural language search works even in air-gapped environments without external APIs.

item.title: Stop Giving Coding Instructions -- Borrow an Expert's Perspective Instead
item.description: Repeating ‘fix this’ only circles within problems you already know. Deploying expert personas reveals blind spots, and learning when to call which persona also saves tokens.

item.title: Overcoming NotebookLM's Generation Limits with Script-Based Control
item.description: When NotebookLM stops at 20 slides, most people just give up. Designate a single master script as the source and push through with code-style commands to generate 40 slides.

item.title: Building an Organic AI Collaboration Flow from Planning to Visualization
item.description: How assigning Claude, Perplexity, and NotebookLM to distinct stages of lecture planning changes the workflow. The point is not using more tools but knowing which tool fits which stage.

item.title: The Unexpected Walls When Converting Web Pages with defuddle
item.description: Tried extracting web data for a RAG pipeline with defuddle and found results vary wildly by site structure. On sites where semantic HTML has collapsed, body text and ads mix together, and in dynamic rendering environments the content itself vanishes.

item.title: Practical Agent Design Lessons from the OpenAI Guide
item.description: Letting an agent handle planning end-to-end causes it to loop and stall in production. The OpenAI guide addresses this with explicit workflow control.

item.title: Directory Design and SOPs That Make or Break AI Agent Organizations
item.description: When you first deploy AI agents, outputs land in random folders and formats vary every time. Aligning folder structure to the org chart and codifying SOPs is what makes quality consistent.

item.title: Cutting Through Claude Code Setup Confusion with Plugin Advisor
item.description: If you have ever stalled in front of the plugin list when first setting up Claude Code, Plugin Advisor narrows that entrance. Preset Packs and pre-flight checklists catch runtime errors before they happen.

item.title: Automating Business Reports with a Multi-AI Pipeline
item.description: Connecting NotebookLM, Claude, and Gemini as a pipeline to turn reports into slides. A single model struggles to handle structuring and formatting simultaneously – splitting roles produces better results at each stage.

item.title: Hands-On Notebooks for the Updated LangChain Ecosystem
item.description: With LangChain’s move to v1, legacy chain code has become outdated. A repository of Korean Jupyter notebooks walks through LangGraph’s State and Node design step by step.

item.title: Automating Tedious Metric Collection with Python and GitHub Actions
item.description: Examining an automated reporting system built with Python and GitHub Actions to escape the tedium of manually checking macroeconomic indicators every day.

item.title: Automating High-Quality HTML Slides with AI Agents and make-slide
item.description: Analyzing a workflow where an AI agent trained on design principles generates polished presentation materials as a single HTML file.

item.title: Data Structure Design for Efficient n8n Workflows
item.description: Addressing data bottlenecks encountered during API integrations and applying n8n’s core mechanics to real-world practice.

item.title: How Data Preprocessing Determines RAG Quality and Leveraging Markdown Conversion Tools
item.description: Examining key features of a Python-based document conversion tool released by a global IT company, and proposing efficient approaches to handling data in LLM pipelines.

item.title: Legal Data Gets a Brain: The Synergy of korean-law-mcp and DataNexus
item.description: korean-law-mcp streamlines legal data access while DataNexus’s knowledge-graph-based ontology layer explicitly maps complex statutory connections, significantly enhancing AI’s ability to interpret law accurately.

item.title: 2. Each AI Cites Different Sources
item.description: ChatGPT favors Wikipedia, Perplexity leans on Reddit, and Gemini prefers official websites. Covering all AI platforms with a single strategy is impossible.

item.title: 1. What Is GEO - AI Citation Strategy Beyond SEO
item.description: Only 9% of Google’s top 10 pages are cited by AI. In an era where SEO rankings no longer guarantee AI citations, we break down the three core principles of GEO and their academic foundations.

item.title: 4. Super-Sub Types — Can a Customer Be Both Individual and Corporate?
item.description: Super-sub types clarify business classifications at the logical model level. When converting to a physical model, three options emerge — and in a DW, that choice reshapes the entire dimension design.

item.title: 3. ERD Notation — Same Diagram, Different Interpretation
item.description: Same Crow’s Foot, different meaning. A single dashed line means different things in different tools. If you want models to serve as a shared language on your project, start by aligning on notation.

item.title: 2. OLTP vs DW Models — Different Purpose, Different Design
item.description: Even when the ERDs look similar, the design philosophies are completely different. OLTP is about transactional integrity; DW is about analytical access paths. That difference creates unfamiliar things like Unknown records and point-in-time data.

item.title: 4. Why We Added a SKOS Compatibility Layer
item.description: Why we chose SKOS to connect the DataNexus ontology with external systems. Designing a compatibility layer between LPG and RDF – two different graph models.

item.title: 1. Is Kimball Still Relevant in the Cloud DW Era?
item.description: How DW modeling considerations have shifted with Synapse, BigQuery, and Redshift. Kimball, Data Vault, One Big Table — practical criteria for choosing the right approach.

item.title: 3. Can DataHub's Glossary Work as an Ontology?
item.description: We tried using DataHub’s Business Glossary as an ontology store. What worked, what didn’t, and how we worked around it.

item.title: 2. How We Chose These 4 Open-Source Tools
item.description: How we decided on DataHub + Vanna + ApeRAG + DozerDB for DataNexus. What got eliminated from the candidate list, and why.

item.title: 1. Why We're Building DataNexus
item.description: The structural problems with enterprise data analytics, and why we decided to build an ontology-based data agent platform.

item.title: About
item.description: Junho Lee - Data & AI Platform Architect

item.title: Development Roadmap
item.description: DataNexus enterprise ontology-based NL2SQL autonomous data agent platform development roadmap and real-time progress
